title: Stephen <> Kevin
summary:
The meeting reviewed operational updates for a new lead and sales workflow, assessed current personnel capacity, and set short-term priorities for closing initial deals. Stephen Polich reported heavy travel and tax-season workload that is limiting capacity, and the team agreed to revisit workload limits midweek; a conference room participant warned about sleep-related cognitive decline and recommended reducing Stephen’s active tasks.

Discussion shifted to vendor lead progress and Sam’s closing performance. Stephen shared a promising vendor conversation and confirmed a go-live plan for two new smart lists pending a Mitch check; the group prioritized helping Sam close a first deal within about 14 days to build momentum and identified objection handling as the main bottleneck. The team proposed an AI-driven objection-practice tool, focused rep support for Sam, and a prototype training/test for objection handling.

Operational topics included lead filtering, discrepancies in Hatch report counts for several sources, and tagging practices for refunded or ineligible leads. Attendees confirmed most of Sam’s leads have been touched, agreed to audit Smart Financial inbound records for missing fields, and committed to documenting a single source of truth for tags and integrations so Stephen can move toward agency oversight rather than hands-on execution.
action_items: Conference room participant and Stephen will revisit Stephen’s workload and potential task reductions on Wednesday
Stephen Polich will research the next company on the vendor list this week
Stephen Polich will take a deeper dive into lead scripting to identify disconnects
Mitch will perform a programming/scripting double-check for the two new smart lists within about two hours
Stephen Polich will sit with Samantha and listen to her calls to support closing a deal by the end of the month
Conference room participant will prototype and test an AI-driven objection handling training for Samantha
Conference room participant will ask Sam to review and clean the FE Kaboom filter tags (remove redundant entries and ensure Smart Financial/Enzone are included).
Conference room participant will consult Mitch about tag configurations and advise whether leads should be dropped or retagged to avoid start/end-of-day cycles.
Stephen Polich will audit inbound Smart Financial leads and add missing DOB and address information where needed.
Mitch will investigate the Smart Financial-to-Hatch integration ("hook") and report back on why DOB and address fields are not reliably transferring.
Conference room participant will check Sam's tag usage and decide whether to manually fix records or return them to Stephen if not resolvable.
Stephen Polich will require Sam to produce a weekly schedule/report (e.g., Notion screenshot) every Friday and make her responsible for that closing report
Conference room participant will send Stephen a short recap email and quick principles for delegation and leveling up from hustler to executive
Conference room participant will document the current tags and explain their purpose and usage for Stephen
Conference room participant will check with Mitch to ensure the related workflows are turned on today
Conference room participant will send instructions on how to think about delegation and present a recommended cadence for system reviews
Conference room participant will continue work on the Medicare workflows discussed during the meeting
Conference room participant will investigate an AI sales trainer focused on handling objections
Stephen Polich will take the recap email and outline workflows he can delegate and workflows he cannot delegate
key_questions: Should the team have a conversation about saying no to some tasks given Stephen’s workload?
Are we going live today with the two new smart lists and is scripting ready?
Is Samantha still practicing and improving objection handling?
On a scale of 1–10, how confident is Stephen that Samantha is doing what she’s supposed to throughout the day?
How should refunded or credited inbound Smart Financial leads be handled so they don't appear in start/end-of-day cycles?
If Smart Financial contains DOB and address, should that data come into Hatch automatically through the integration ("hook")?
Can someone help clarify the purpose of specific tags?
Anything else to go over before closing?
